programs.name: Iowa Medicaid Home & Community Based Services Elderly Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Provides Medicaid-funded long-term services and supports for elderly residents at risk of nursing home admission, helping them remain in their homes and communities. Services include personal emergency response systems, homemaker services, personal care, adult day care, respite care, home modifications, home-delivered meals, and consumer-directed options with a monthly budget.
programs.who_its_for: Iowa residents aged 65 or older who are Medicaid-eligible, meet nursing facility level of care requirements, and have income up to $2,982/month (300% of Federal Benefit Rate in 2026).
programs.whats_unique: State-administered Medicaid waiver program tailored as a nursing home diversion with consumer choice options and self-directed financial management services, not a standard federal program available uniformly across states.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by individual needs; includes specific services like home modifications (e.g., ramps, grab bars), respite care, and monthly consumer budget for self-directed care.

programs.name: HCBS Waivers (e.g., Aged and Disabled Waiver)
programs.what_it_does: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) waivers allow seniors and disabled individuals to receive long-term care services in their homes or community instead of nursing homes. Services include personal care, homemaker support, and adult day care administered through Iowa HHS.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals who meet nursing facility level of care and income eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Annual costs up to $60,000+ per person depending on needs; prevents institutionalization.

programs.name: Iowa PACE
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health and transportation. It is offered through select Iowa providers under federal guidelines.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ who are nursing home eligible, live in PACE service areas, and can reside safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage; average annual value $50,000+ per enrollee.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. In Iowa, administered as part of Medicaid, with QMB covering Part A/B premiums fully, SLMB Part B, and QI partial Part B.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+ meeting specific income/asset limits (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: up to $2,900/year savings; SLMB/QI: $80-185/month Part B premium coverage.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy nutritious food. Iowa administers it through HHS with online application and local offices.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (60+) with income typically under 130% FPL; simplified rules for elderly/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per household; max $291 for one person (2026 rates).

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP helps with heating and cooling bills, crisis assistance, and weatherization referrals. Iowa's version is administered via HHS with applications online or local offices.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households, prioritizing seniors, disabled, and families with young children (income ~150% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-1,000 depending on fuel type and need.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs for low-income homes. Administered in Iowa through community action agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and families with children (200% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Free services; average savings $300/year on energy bills.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free, personalized Medicare counseling, plan comparisons, and assistance with appeals. Iowa's program is state-funded and federally supported.
programs.who_its_for: All Iowa Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors navigating options.
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling; helps save thousands on premiums/drugs annually.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via Aging Services)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Administered through Iowa's Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income.
programs.benefit_value: Suggested donation; full cost $7-10/meal, serves 1M+ meals/year statewide.

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, and support to family caregivers of seniors. Iowa delivers via Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of adults 60+ or grandparents raising children.
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers/grants up to $1,600/year for respite; training free.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service jobs with training for low-income seniors. Iowa partners with Experience Works and others.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$15/hr), 20 hrs/week for up to 48 months.

programs.name: Iowa Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for benefit issues, housing, and elder rights. Includes benefits screening via BenefitsCheckUp.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing legal barriers to benefits/services.
programs.benefit_value: Free services; resolves issues worth thousands in benefits.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Administered by Iowa Department of Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities in Iowa.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80%+ complaints without escalation.

programs.name: State Supplementary Assistance (SSA)
programs.what_it_does: Fully state-funded program supplementing SSI for aged, blind, and disabled individuals to cover special needs not met by federal SSI benefits. Covers categories including blind allowance, dependent person allowance, family life home assistance, in-home health-related care, residential care facility assistance, and supplements for Medicare/Medicaid eligible.
programs.who_its_for: Aged, blind, or disabled Iowa SSI recipients with additional special needs in the specified categories.
programs.whats_unique: Entirely state-funded supplement to federal SSI with Iowa-specific categories and payment rates (e.g., Family Home Life payment to family: $1,026; Dependent Person max: $518; Residential Care Facility per diem up to $38.47), not available in every state.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by category: Family Life Home $1,156 standard; Dependent Person up to $518 (or $1,512-$3,031 with dependents); Residential Care Facility $130 personal needs + $17.86-$38.47 per diem.
